nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia runs the indie label L7 Records with friends from her former hip-hop collective
nuggets.text: Before her solo career, Cornelia was part of a hip-hop collective called Visibly Inflight. Today, she and the other members of that group run their own indie label, L7 Records. The name comes from a store she wanted to open for her high school clothing company in Marietta Square.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.publisher: Voyage ATL Magazine | ATL City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: me and the friends of the group I use to be in called Visibly Inflight run our own indie label “L7 Records” named after this store I wanted to open up for my clothing company back in high school at Marietta Square.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, a multi-disciplinary artist and co-founder of L7 Records.

nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia’s entry into music videos came from being the worst skater on her team
nuggets.text: At 15, Cornelia started a clothing company to sponsor her skate team. Since she wasn't the best skater, she took on the role of videographer, using free cameras from her high school's AFJROTC program to film skate lines for YouTube. This experience led her to become the go-to music video director in Marietta after the Odd Future wave inspired her friends to start rapping.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.publisher: Voyage ATL Magazine | ATL City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I became the videographer for the team since I wasn’t the best skater on the team and I had access to free cameras in AFJROTC in high school. Then, Odd Future happened and all my friends became rappers...and that’s how started filming music videos

nuggets.imageCaption: in Marietta after the Odd Future wave inspired her friends to start rapping

nuggets.headline: If you're connecting with Jamee Cornelia, listen to Yani Mo next
nuggets.text: Both Jamee Cornelia and fellow artist Yani Mo are fixtures of the vibrant Atlanta-area independent hip-hop scene. While Cornelia hails from Marietta, Mo is from nearby Lithonia, and their work draws from the same regional creative energy. Start with Yani Mo's album "The Current."

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.publisher: Voyage ATL Magazine | ATL City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I became the go-to music video director in my area (Marietta and parts of Atlanta that were Marta accessible, lol!).

nuggets.imageCaption: Atlanta-area artist Yani Mo, a contemporary of Jamee Cornelia.

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a multi-talented artist from Marietta, GA, who got her start filming skate videos before becoming a musician, director, and co-founder of the indie label L7 Records. Her work is rooted in a DIY ethos, blending hip-hop with a background in punk rock.
